questions.prompt: A clothing store buys shirts at a wholesale price of \$6.00 each and resells them at a retail price that is 310% of the wholesale price. questions.testName: December 2024 International Form A SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Raheem bought 9 shirts that were each the same price. He used a coupon for \$54 off the entire purchase. The cost for the entire purchase after using the coupon was \$108. What was the original price, in dollars, for 1 shirt?
questions.questionText: Raheem bought 9 shirts that were each the same price. He used a coupon for \$54 off the entire purchase. The cost for the entire purchase after using the coupon was \$108. What was the original price, in dollars, for 1 shirt?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: A sample of a certain isotope takes 29 years to decay to half its original mass. The function $s(t)=136(0.5)^{\frac{t}{29}}$ gives the approximate mass of this isotope, in grams, that remains $t$ years after a 136 gram sample starts to decay. Which statement is the best interpretation of $s(58)= 34$ in this context?
questions.questionText: A sample of a certain isotope takes 29 years to decay to half its original mass. The function $s(t)=136(0.5)^{\frac{t}{29}}$ gives the approximate mass of this isotope, in grams, that remains $t$ years after a 136 gram sample starts to decay. Which statement is the best interpretation of $s(58)= 34$ in this context?

choices.text: Approximately 34 grams of the sample remains 58 years after the sample starts to decay.
The mass of the sample has decreased by approximately 34 grams 58 years after the sample starts to decay.
The mass of the sample has decreased by approximately 58 grams 34 years after the sample starts to decay.
Approximately 58 grams of the sample remains 34 years after the sample starts to decay.

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form E SAT Math Module 1
questions.prompt: Antonia has \$130 in an account. Each year she expects to have $2.5\%$ more money in the account than she had the previous year. Which of the following models best describes how Antonia expects the money in her account to change over time?
questions.questionText: Antonia has \$130 in an account. Each year she expects to have $2.5\%$ more money in the account than she had the previous year. Which of the following models best describes how Antonia expects the money in her account to change over time?

questions.testName: March 2025 US Form A SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: The function $f$ is defined by $f(x) = 6x - \frac{1}{8}$. What is the $y$-intercept of the graph of $y = f(x)$ in the $xy$-plane?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$700 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $16h+13c=700$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 16 in this context?
questions.questionText: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$700 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $16h+13c=700$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 16 in this context?

choices.text: The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her regular job
The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her second job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her regular job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her second job

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: At a convention center, there are a total of 375 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is $0.64$, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is $0.32$. How many visitors are located in room C?
questions.questionText: At a convention center, there are a total of 375 visitors. Each visitor is located in either room A, room B, or room C. If one of these visitors is selected at random, the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room A is $0.64$, and the probability of selecting a visitor who is located in room B is $0.32$. How many visitors are located in room C?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A researcher observes a sample of a nuclide. An exponential model estimates that the mass, in grams, of the sample decreases by 22% every 11.11 minutes. Which of the following equations could represent this model, where $M$ is the estimated mass, in grams, of the sample $t$ minutes after the researcher began observing the sample?
questions.questionText: A researcher observes a sample of a nuclide. An exponential model estimates that the mass, in grams, of the sample decreases by 22% every 11.11 minutes. Which of the following equations could represent this model, where $M$ is the estimated mass, in grams, of the sample $t$ minutes after the researcher began observing the sample?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: Line k is defined by $y=8x+7$. Line j is parallel to line k in the $xy$-plane and passes through the point $(0, 15)$. Which equation defines line j?
questions.questionText: Line k is defined by $y=8x+7$. Line j is parallel to line k in the $xy$-plane and passes through the point $(0, 15)$. Which equation defines line j?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: For the linear function $p$, $p(c)=-6$, where $c$ is a constant, $p(5)=42$, and the slope of the graph of $y=p(x)$ in the $xy$-plane is 8. For the linear function $t$, $t(c)=-7$ and $t(6)=56$. What is the slope of the graph of $y=t(x)$ in the $xy$-plane?
questions.questionText: For the linear function $p$, $p(c)=-6$, where $c$ is a constant, $p(5)=42$, and the slope of the graph of $y=p(x)$ in the $xy$-plane is 8. For the linear function $t$, $t(c)=-7$ and $t(6)=56$. What is the slope of the graph of $y=t(x)$ in the $xy$-plane?

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form C SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: A square map has a side length of 55 inches, and 1 inch on the map represents an actual distance of 13 miles. On the smaller map, which of the following is closest to the actual distance, in miles, represented by 1 inch?

questions.prompt: Right rectangular prism $X$ is similar to right rectangular prism $Y$. The surface area of right rectangular prism $X$ is 59 square centimeters ($\text{cm}^2$), and the surface area of right rectangular prism $Y$ is 1,475 $\text{cm}^2$. The volume of right rectangular prism $Y$ is 1,500 cubic centimeters ($\text{cm}^3$). What is the sum of the volumes, in $\text{cm}^3$, of right rectangular prism $X$ and right rectangular prism $Y$?

questions.questionText: A sample of a certain isotope takes 29 years to decay to half its original mass. The function $s(t)=136(0.5)^{\frac{t}{29}}$ gives the approximate mass of this isotope, in grams, that remains $t$ years after a 136 gram sample starts to decay. Which statement is the best interpretation of $s(58)= 34$ in this context?

choices.text: Approximately 34 grams of the sample remains 58 years after the sample starts to decay.
The mass of the sample has decreased by approximately 34 grams 58 years after the sample starts to decay.
The mass of the sample has decreased by approximately 58 grams 34 years after the sample starts to decay.
Approximately 58 grams of the sample remains 34 years after the sample starts to decay.

questions.testName: March 2025 International Form B SAT Math Module 2
questions.prompt: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$700 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $16h+13c=700$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 16 in this context?
questions.questionText: In one week in 2017, a technician earned a total of \$700 by working at her regular job and at a second job doing part-time work. The equation $16h+13c=700$ represents this situation, where $h$ is the number of hours worked at her regular job and $c$ is the number of hours worked at her second job. Which of the following is the best interpretation of 16 in this context?

choices.text: The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her regular job
The amount, in dollars, the technician earned for each hour she worked at her second job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her regular job
The number of hours the technician worked in one week at her second job
